figures.caption: FIG. 3. (a) A↑(k, ω = 0) for the perturbed HK Hamiltonian in the ld-p phase, i.e., p-wave magnetism in the ld regime (U = −1.0, U ′ = 9.0). (b) Same quantity for the ld-FM state, with interactions U = −1.75, U ′ = 20.0. (c) Phase diagram of the different magnetic phases, defined by the combination of the type of S1 region for the underlying unperturbed HK model (ld, lv, ring, and disk) and the form of magnetic order (FM, d, and p) obtained through energetic minimization. Throughout, we chose µ = −1.0, t2 = 0.5.

FIG. 5. Different spin configurations (a-d) of ring and lv S1, that are obtained from the minimization, and their corresponding spin up spectral functions A↑(k, ω = 0) (e-h). Combined with the spectral functions shown in the long range order section of the main text, the shown spectral functions give examples for all phases in the phase diagram Fig. 3c.

FIG. 6. Spin configurations of the three magnetic phases in the ring regime of the single-orbtial HK model and their corresponding spin up spectral functions A↑(k, ω = 0). The single-orbital HK model only allows ring and disk S1. Therefore, the spectral functions shown constitute a comprehensive set of non-trivial spectral functions.

FIG. 1. (a) Checkerboard lattice with two sublattices (◦ = A; • = B), nearest (t1) and next-nearest neighbor hopping (t2). (b) Momentum-space occupation of the ground state of HHK in the ld regime, for U = −1.0, U ′ = 4.0. (c) Phase diagram of the different ground state occupation configurations ld, lv and ring (see main text and insets for definition). We took µ = −1.0 and t2 = 0.5.

FIG. 4. (a) Spectral function Aσ(k, ω) of HHK + δH2S along high symmetry paths in k space and (b) its momentum dependence at ω = 0, i.e., Aσ(k, ω = 0). Note that the perturbation δH2S leads to the emergence of a gap but does not break spin-rotation symmetry, A↑ = A↓ = Aσ. Both spectral functions are computed at finite temperature T = 0.05 and coupling J = −1. The parameters of the bare HK model are chosen to match those of the spectral function in Fig. 2c.
